law: Use a model as judge to score usefulness and accuracy of the tags.
These checks are cheap and deterministic: they run quickly and always return the same result for the same input.

The test reads the aggregated result from and asserts a threshold, which turns a quality target into a pass-or-fail check in the test suite: In the sample’s evaluation, the threshold for the metric requires at least 80 percent of the runs to produce a valid tag count.
The test requires a score above a threshold: In the sample, the test requires a Cohen’s kappa above 0.6 on both the relevance and usefulness dimensions.
Expectations can be unordered or ordered, can allow extra calls, can match arguments exactly or by natural-language criteria, and can forbid tools a correct answer must never call.
This ordered expectation requires the assistant to search by tag and then fetch the matching book’s details: An argument doesn’t always have to match a fixed string.
An expectation can also name tools the assistant must not call.
Here the assistant should narrow by genre and never fall back to a similarity search: A scores each trajectory, reporting whether every expected call passed and what percentage passed.
The test then asserts a minimum pass rate: For the full set of argument matchers and trajectory options, see .
